title: Math Kangaroo USA 2011 — Grades 3-4
disclaimer: © Math Kangaroo USA, NFP, Inc. All rights reserved. Used for personal educational practice only.

questions.question_text: Bernd wants to paint the word KANGAROO. He begins on a Wednesday and paints one letter each day. On which day will he paint the last letter?
questions.solution: KANGAROO has 8 letters. The 2nd O is the 8th letter. Starting Wednesday (Day 1): Day 8 = Wednesday + 7 days = Wednesday. Answer: Wednesday (A).
solution_steps: K(1-Wed) A(2-Thu) N(3-Fri) G(4-Sat) A(5-Sun) R(6-Mon) O(7-Tue) O(8-Wed).
The 8th letter is painted on Day 8 = Wednesday.

questions.question_text: Which stone should Mr Flintstone place on the right side of the scales, so that both sides weigh the same?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for the figure. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: A game is played on a board as shown in the picture. I move the counter from square to square according to the following rules. First, one square to the right, then one square up, then one square left, then one square down, and then once again one square right. Which picture shows where the counter can then be found?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for the figure. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: Simon awoke one and a half hours ago. In three and a half hours he will catch a train to go to his grandma. How long before the time his train leaves, did he wake up?
questions.solution: Awake for 1.5 hours already + 3.5 hours remaining = 5 hours total awake time today. Answer: 5 hours (E).

questions.question_text: Maria describes one of these five shapes in the following way: „It is not a square. It is grey. It is either round or three sided.“ Which shape did she describe?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for the figure. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: Lenka paid 1 Euro and 50 Cents for three scoops of ice cream. Miso paid 2 Euro’s and 40 Cents for two chocolate bars. How much did Igor pay for one scoop of ice cream and one chocolate bar?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for this question. Correct answer: A.

questions.question_text: The bell of a clocktower rings every full hour (8:00, 9:00, 10:00 etc.) and rings as many times as the number of hours. It also rings once on every half hour (8:30, 9:30, 10:30 etc). How often will it ring between 7:55 and 10:45?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for the figure. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: Which shape has the biggest area?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for the figure. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: A chicken farmer packs eggs in boxes of 6 and boxes of 12. What is the smallest number of boxes he needs to pack 66 eggs?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for this question. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: All the children in a class at school have at least one pet, and at most two pets. They write down how many pets they have together. Two children each have a dog and a fish. Three have each a cat and a dog. No child has two cats. Altogether they have eight cats, six dogs, and two fish. How many children are in the class?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for this question. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: Johannes has only 5 Cent coins and 10 Cent coins in his pocket. Altogether he has 13 coins. Which of the following amounts cannot be the total of his coins?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for the figure. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: A page is folded along the thick line as shown. Which letter will not be covered by a grey square?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for the figure. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: Anna, Bob, Cleo, Dido, Eva, and Ferdl each roll a dice. Each person rolls a different number. Anna’s number is twice as big as Bob’s. Anna’s number is three times as big as Cleo’s. Dido’s number is four times as big as Eva’s. Which number did Ferdl roll?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for this question. Correct answer: E.

questions.question_text: A quizshow has the following rules: Each contestant begins with 10 points. They must answer 10 questions. For each correct answer they get a point and for each incorrect answer they lose a point. Mrs Blandorfer finished the show with 14 points. How many questions had she answered incorrectly?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for the figure. Correct answer: A.

questions.question_text: In each square of the maze there is a piece of cheese. Ronnie the mouse wants to enter and leave the maze as shown in the picture. He doesn’t want to visit a square more than once, but would like to eat as much cheese as possible. What is the maximum number of pieces of cheese that he can eat?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for the figure. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: During a party, two identical cakes were each cut into four identical pieces. Each of these pieces was then cut into three identical pieces. Each person at the party got a piece of cake, and there were three pieces left over. How many people were at the party?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for the figure. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: Four friends Masha, Sasha, Dasha and Pasha are sitting on a bench. At first Masha swapped places with Dasha. Then Dasha swapped places with Pasha. After this the four friends are sitting from left to right in the order: Masha, Sasha, Dasha, Pasha. In what order, from left to right were they sitting to begin with?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for this question. Correct answer: A.

questions.question_text: How often in a day does a digital clock display four identical digits? The picture shows a digital clock that is displaying exactly two different digits.
questions.solution: See PDF page 4 for the figure. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: Four identical dice were put together to make a tower as shown. The sum of the numbers on opposite faces of each dice is always 7. What would the tower look like from behind?
questions.solution: See PDF page 4 for the figure. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: You can place together the cards pictured, to make different three digit numbers, for instance 989 or 986. How many different three digit numbers can you make with these cards?
questions.solution: See PDF page 4 for the figure. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: Andrea made the pattern in the picture out of several identical tiles. None of the tiles overlap each other. Which of the following tiles could she definitely not have used?
questions.solution: See PDF page 4 for the figure. Correct answer: E.

questions.question_text: The picture shows a Fortress made from cubes. How many cubes were used to make it?
questions.solution: See PDF page 4 for the figure. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: Johannes wrote the numbers 6, 7 and 8 in the circles as shown. He wants to write the numbers 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5 in the remaining circles so that the sum of the numbers along each side of the square is 13. What will be the sum of the numbers in the grey circles?
questions.solution: See PDF page 4 for the figure. Correct answer: A.

questions.question_text: Sylvia draws patterns with hexagons as shown. If she carries on drawing in this way, how many hexagons will there be in the fifth pattern?
questions.solution: See PDF page 4 for the figure. Correct answer: E.
